title: Structures, Unions, Tagged Variants, and Flexible Arrays · C Memory Workbench
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working tagged packet representation while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of tagged packet representation while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Initialize, copy, compare, and validate structures by members and invariants rather than raw-byte assumptions.
Pair a union with an explicit discriminant and access only the active, validated member.
Allocate flexible-array objects with checked header-plus-elements arithmetic and record their element count.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Aggregate Invariants
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 6535. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a tagged payload and its allocation size; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Initialize, copy, compare, and validate structures by members and invariants rather than raw-byte assumptions.

hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Opaque Structure Evolution, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Opaque Structure Evolution precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Opaque Structure Evolution, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 5332. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a tagged payload and its allocation size; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Hide representation behind incomplete types and constructor, observer, mutation, and destructor contracts.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Opaque Structure Evolution, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: Publishing structure fields cannot constrain future ABI-compatible changes.

stimulus.title: Opaque Structure Evolution failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Opaque Structure Evolution case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Opaque Structure Evolution condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Opaque Structure Evolution
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Opaque Structure Evolution: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 6951. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a tagged payload and its allocation size; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Hide representation behind incomplete types and constructor, observer, mutation, and destructor contracts.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Opaque Structure Evolution.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Opaque Structure Evolution, keep Opaque Structure Evolution connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Hide representation behind incomplete types and constructor, observer, mutation, and destructor contracts.
